archimate:Representation.name: Turtle
archimate:Representation.documentation:
Description: Turtle (Terse RDF Triple Language) is a human-readable serialization format for representing RDF (Resource Description Framework) data. It provides a concise and compact syntax to express triples, which consist of subject-predicate-object statements. Turtle is widely used for creating and sharing RDF data due to its simplicity and readability.
Publisher: W3C
Source: https://www.w3.org/TR/turtle/
LOST view: SV-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/TurtleRepresentation
EIRA concept: eira:SolutionBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/TurtleRepresentation
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Realises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataRepresentation

properties.value: Turtle (Terse RDF Triple Language) is a human-readable serialization format for representing RDF (Resource Description Framework) data. It provides a concise and compact syntax to express triples, which consist of subject-predicate-object statements. Turtle is widely used for creating and sharing RDF data due to its simplicity and readability.
